text: What you have done is rarer than people say: not merely the stopping, but the willingness to become someone the old self wouldn't quite recognize. Every habit is a room you've furnished over years, and you walked out without taking the furniture. There's grief in that, even in triumph. But now you carry the proof — quiet, internal, unannounced — that you are not simply the sum of your accumulated patterns. The architecture of the self turns out to be more malleable than we feared. I'm genuinely proud of you.
